# CrossWindowDragTarget: the receiving-window contract implementation

## Context
Tree line **C1** (#13158 map) — the T-C tranche gate and a fable-tier leaf: implementing ADR 0029 §2.3's `CrossWindowDragTarget` contract (formalized verbatim in the spec from the #13370 Option-4 convergence): how a window RECEIVES a drag that originated in a sibling window on the same App-Worker heap.

## The Problem
Cross-window drag is the only-Neo capability (single heap, multiple Chromium windows) and the highest-risk interaction surface: the receiving window must render previews for an item it doesn't host yet, negotiate drop zones through the SAME preview→operation path as local drags (no parallel drag system — the inherited hard guardrail), and hand off atomically.

## The Architectural Reality
- §2.3's contract: the target registers per-window; drag state is worker-owned shared truth (`get_drag_state`'s substrate); previews are per-window projections; the drop commits ONE `transferItem`-class operation (C3's leaf) through the executor.
- Boundary: OS-window creation/reintegration stays #13028's (consumed, not owned).
- Placement: `src/dashboard/` beside the landed drag preview machinery (sibling fast-path).

## The Fix
One PR: the target implementation (registration, remote-drag preview binding, drop negotiation → operation descriptor emission) + unit specs (registration lifecycle; preview binds worker drag state; drop emits the exact §2.3 descriptor shape; cancel path clean).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Contract shape matches §2.3's formalization field-for-field (reviewer table).
- [ ] Previews ride the EXISTING preview machinery (no parallel path — review-checkable).
- [ ] Drop emits one descriptor; no direct document mutation (executor discipline).
- [ ] Cancel/escape leaves both windows' projections clean (spec).
- [ ] Cross-family review.

## Out of Scope
`transferItem` op wiring (C3) · edge affordances (C4) · window spawning (#13028) · demo scene (G3).

## Related
Parent #13158 (line C1) · authority ADR 0029 §2.3 · consumes worker drag-state substrate · consumed by C2-C4, #14590. Sweeps: 03:47Z clean.

- 2026-07-04T12:50:35Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `1a2fe03` - "feat(dashboard): CrossWindowDragTarget — the harness-docking §2.3 receiving-window contract (#14670)

The thin target a dock workspace registers with Neo.manager.DragCoordinator to receive
cross-window drags: §2.3 registry identity (sortGroup/windowId) + the four mandatory hooks,
with ALL dock semantics arriving through owner-supplied seams (hitTest / previewFor /
clearPreview / previewToOperation / commitOperation) — no parallel drag system, the
coordinator stays dock-blind, and src/dashboard imports no app-layer preview renderer.

Fail-closed on every path: no hitTest seam → never claims the drag; no accepted preview or a
converter rejection → drop commits nothing; the drop consumes the preview so hover state never
leaks past the gesture. transferItem mapping stays the committing adapter's job (C3 boundary,
consumed not owned).

6 unit specs: registration lifecycle (register on complete identity, unregister on destroy,
incomplete identity never registers), fail-closed + delegating hit-test, hover stores the
owner preview + leave clears through the owner seam, drop rides the one
previewToOperation→commitOperation pipeline with draggedItem context, no-preview/rejected
drops commit nothing."
- 2026-07-04T12:51:38Z @neo-fable-clio cross-referenced by PR #14757
- 2026-07-04T13:09:53Z @neo-fable-clio referenced in commit `1af9102` - "fix(dashboard): unconditional drop-path cleanup — a throwing owner commit never strands hover state (#14670)

Discharges Euclid's pre-review falsifier (IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABIv72_g): onRemoteDrop left
currentPreview set and skipped clearPreview when commitOperation threw, contradicting the
fail-closed cleanup claim. try/finally per his suggested shape: the owner's error still
propagates (its bug to observe), the gesture still ends clean. Regression pins throw +
currentPreview null + clearPreview fired."
